page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.large is a compute-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

The underlying x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, and AVX-512 Vector Neural Network Instructions (VNNI) for accelerated vector and deep learning calculations. Cryptographic operations are aided by AES, VAES, and SHA_NI instruction support. The CPU configuration allocates dedicated resources with one thread per core, ensuring consistent compute performance without hyper-threading resource sharing.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1874.59 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1487.0. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics fall into the lower percentiles, as evidenced by a multi-core Geekbench score of 1742.0 and uncached read memory bandwidth of 14.32 GB/sec. The instance is suited for lightweight compute tasks, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, and small-scale text processing workloads that do not require high memory density or local storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c7.large is a compute-optimized virtual server featuring 2 dedicated vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8369B processor at 2.7 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via KVM, this instance provides a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio with no local storage or GPU accelerators. It supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instruction sets like AES and SHA_NI. Benchmark results show average single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1874.59 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1487.0, though multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are relatively low. With a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, this instance is designed for lightweight, single-threaded compute tasks, basic web serving, and small-scale text processing workloads.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core compute instance with AVX-512 support, designed for lightweight single-threaded processing and basic web serving workloads.

4.0 GB of system memory providing a 1:2 compute-to-RAM ratio
Supports AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AES cryptographic instruction sets
